meaning: n.偶然,偶然事件adj.偶然的,随便的adv.偶然地
examples: Could the same approach also shed light on abstract twentieth-century pieces, from Mondrian's geometrical blocks of colour, to Pollock's seemingly haphazard arrangements of splashed paint on canvas?
The books had been piled on the shelves in a haphazard fashion.
Before Smell-O-Vision, other motion pictures had unsuccessfully tried to use aromas in disjointed and haphazard ways.
PRI's efforts to woo young voters, who shunned it in the presidential election, are equally haphazard.
